codeReview.summary: Solid implementation that covers all four requirements: server proxy endpoint with validation, UI input with Load/Reset buttons, localStorage persistence with auto-fetch on page load, and 9 test cases. The proxy function uses streaming body reads with a 5MB limit, validates HTTPS-only URLs, checks JSON structure for a tokens array, and applies a 30-second fetch timeout. UI code properly uses textContent (not innerHTML) to display messages, preventing XSS. Error handling covers all specified cases with correct HTTP status codes. The client-side code includes Enter-key support, autocomplete refresh after token loading, and graceful fallback to default tokenlist if saved URL fails on reload.

issues.description: SSRF risk: The proxy endpoint fetches any HTTPS URL without filtering for private/internal IP ranges (e.g., 10.x, 172.16.x, 192.168.x, 127.0.0.1, ::1). An attacker could use this to probe internal services that have HTTPS endpoints. For a developer tool this is low risk, but worth noting for future hardening.

No observability on proxy endpoint: Other endpoints (/tokenlist, /quote, /compare) call logError(), recordRequest(), and trackError() for monitoring. The /tokenlist/proxy endpoint has none of these, making it harder to debug and monitor usage or abuse.

Fragile error-to-status-code mapping: The route handler uses message.includes('Missing url'), message.includes('Invalid URL'), message.includes('HTTPS') to determine 400 vs 502. If error messages in fetchProxyTokenList are ever refactored, the status classification would break silently. A more robust approach would be custom error types or error codes.

4 of 9 proxy tests hit external services (tokens.uniswap.org, example.com) in real time. While the test file is already named 'integration', these create flakiness risk if external services are down or rate-limit. Consider mocking fetch for unit-level tests and keeping a single integration smoke test.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md API Endpoints section does not list the new GET /tokenlist/proxy endpoint. The worker added the endpoint but did not update the documentation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md lists /health, /chains, /compare, /quote, /metrics but not /tokenlist/proxy. Compare to git show fba5758 which adds the route handler at line ~3125.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No convention documented for adding observability (logging, metrics, error tracking) to new endpoints. The proxy endpoint lacks recordRequest(), logError(), and trackError() calls that all other non-trivial endpoints use. If AGENTS.md or the skill documented this pattern, workers would replicate it consistently.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/server.ts: /quote uses recordRequest() at lines 3173,3190 and trackError() at 3199; /compare uses them at 3222,3230,3231; /tokenlist uses logError() at 3118. The new /tokenlist/proxy at 3125-3152 has none.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No security guidance for server-side proxy endpoints (SSRF filtering). The proxy fetches arbitrary HTTPS URLs without private IP filtering. If more proxy endpoints are added, this pattern could proliferate.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/server.ts fetchProxyTokenList() at line ~137 validates only url.protocol === 'https:' but does not check for private/reserved IP ranges.

summary: PASS. The custom-tokenlist-url feature fully implements all four requirements: (1) GET /tokenlist/proxy with HTTPS validation, 5MB streaming limit, JSON+tokens-array validation, and proper 400/502 error codes; (2) UI input with Load/Reset buttons, loading states, and error messages; (3) localStorage persistence with auto-fetch on reload and graceful fallback; (4) 9 integration tests covering all validation paths. Non-blocking issues: no SSRF private-IP filtering, no observability (logging/metrics/error-tracking) on the proxy endpoint, fragile string-based error classification, and external-service-dependent tests. Three shared state observations flagged for AGENTS.md (missing endpoint docs, missing observability convention, missing SSRF guidance).
